items.title: Secret world of state care investigated in parliamentary inquiry

children.content: The state agencies which control the lives of some of Western Australia's most vulnerable people are set to be scrutinised in a parliamentary inquiry, with public submission now open.

items.title: Man, 53, charged over seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants

children.content: A 53-year-old Kalgoorlie man is released on bail after the seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants from a property WA Police allege had been converted for the purpose of growing hydroponic cannabis.

image.altText: Photos of a hydronic cannabis operation supplied by police.

items.title: Containers for Change set to include wine bottles, but industry unhappy

children.content: A newly formed West Australian wine group warns that bottles of wine produced in the state could increase by $1.25 if the expansion of the Containers for Change scheme goes ahead.

items.title: Missing royalties labelled 'petty cash' for Gina Rinehart after court ruling

children.content: Gina Rinehart and her company, Hancock Prospecting, are the "clear winners" of what prominent Perth industry commentator Tim Treadgold has declared the "big daddy" of legal battles.

articles.title: Here's what you need to know about the Gina Rinehart court ruling

children.content: The blockbuster court ruling over mining tenements worth billions of dollars in WA's iron ore rich Pilbara is the culmination of a complicated legal battle spanning more than a decade that has pitted family against family and siblings against each other.

articles.title: Split decision as Gina Rinehart, Wright family both claim legal wins

children.content: Gina Rinehart is dealt a blow, with a court finding the family of her father's former business partner has the right to potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in royalties from lucrative Pilbara mining assets.

items.title: Racist comments on WA Police social media posts remain online for days

children.content: Racist comments are being published on WA Police social media posts with little moderation, but the force says it is not prepared to switch off comments.

gallery.altText: A photo over someone's shoulder of them holding a phone with the WA Police Force facebook page on it.
A blurred head looks at a laptop screen of blurred comments with under the heading 'Western Australia Police Force's Post'.
The WA Police crest on the bonnet of a parked car.

items.title: In charts: A look at Australia's record spending on defence

children.content: The Defence Department reveals where the money will be going as Australia plans to spend a record $425 billion over the next decade.

image.altText: Minister for Defence Industry Pat Conroy and Deputy Prime Minister Richard Marles in front of a submarine.

items.title: What we know about the 'small fire' that became an 'unprecedented' Geelong refinery blaze

children.content: The "unprecedented" fire at one of the two remaining oil refineries in Australia took more than 12 hours to extinguish. Here's what we know about how it unfolded.

items.title: Australia is on the verge of aged care AI boom

children.content: Artificial intelligence-enabled robot companions, personal behaviour monitors and pain management apps could soon be used more widely within the sector.

items.title: Migration debate deserves less politicking from the Liberals

children.content: The Liberals need to resolve the internal ambiguities they have on big policy questions when it comes to migration.

image.altText: Angus Taylor stands behind Jonno Duniam.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown

children.content: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

items.title: AFL-backed helmet company erases concussion protection claims

children.content: An AFL-endorsed helmet company has backtracked on its claims of "superior protection against concussion" and the league is now also facing a complaint lodged with the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission on the matter.

image.altText: A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

items.title: 'Never thought we'd be homeless': Mother of 13 pleads for help

children.content: Finding a home for a large family in a regional area has left one family camping on the side of the highway in rural New South Wales. Nationally, Indigenous people are over-represented in homelessness statistics.

image.altText: A boy eats his noodles in a camp chair.

items.title: Decision looms as Ben Roberts-Smith expected to apply for bail

children.content: Australia's most decorated living soldier has spent more than a week in custody. Today in court, he is expected to make a bid to be released on bail after being charged with war crimes.

image.altText: Ben Roberts Smith in a blue suit, white shirt, outside court on a sunny day.

items.title: Invasive species you should avoid — and what to plant instead

children.content: Agapanthus, morning glory or gazanias might be easy-growing plants in your home but their invasive nature can damage your garden and the environment. Here's what experts recommend.

image.altText: A woman with black curly hair walking through a door outside with a plant pot in hand.

items.title: Food minister warns of challenging times for WA consumers at checkout

children.content: WA Agriculture and Food Minister Jackie Jarvis says natural disasters as well as fuel and fertiliser availability and cost increases are contributing to real concerns around food supply in WA.

items.title: Daicos inspires Magpies to win as Blues youngster misses kick after siren

children.content: Nick Daicos stars in the final quarter to inspire Collingwood to a win as Carlton youngster Talor Byrne misses a kick after the siren to tie the scores.

image.altText: Nick Daicos celebrates a goal with teammates

items.title: How to tell if your dog is in pain and what to do if they are

children.content: New research suggests that many dog owners are not skilled in recognising pain in their pets.

image.altText: A brown dog with floppy ears.

items.title: US alliance indispensable as Marles looks to build more self-sufficient ADF

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles has declared the United States alliance will remain indispensable for Australian national security even while Australia looks to build a more self-reliant defence force.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks at the National Press Club.

items.title: Kerr scores landmark goal as Matildas win FIFA Series

children.content: Sam Kerr's 75th international goal has helped the Matildas to a surprisingly tough, controversial 2-0 victory over Kenya in the FIFA Series final.

items.title: Amid chronic nursing shortages in country WA, Hamish is showing what it's like

children.content: Content creator Hamish Briggs is sharing his journey working as a nurse in Western Australia's far north, as the healthcare sector grapples with workforce shortages in regional areas.
